Union Budget 2026 Impact on Markets.headline: Union Budget 2026: Rs11.1 Lakh Crore Capex Push Reshapes India Growth Trajectory
Union Budget 2026 Impact on Markets.executiveSummary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman delivered a Rs11.1 lakh crore capital expenditure allocation - a 15% increase over FY25 - focused on infrastructure, green energy, and semiconductor manufacturing. The fiscal deficit target of 4.5% of GDP was maintained, surprising markets that had priced in slippage. Income tax relief for the middle class (zero tax up to Rs12 lakh) is expected to inject Rs1.2 lakh crore into consumption. Bond markets rallied 40bps on fiscal discipline; equity markets hit all-time highs within 48 hours of the budget.

keyPoints.point: Infrastructure gets Rs4.2 lakh crore - roads, railways, ports
keyPoints.detail: The allocation covers 50,000 km of new highways, 200 new railway stations, and 3 new greenfield ports. Private sector is expected to co-invest Rs8 lakh crore under PPP mode.

keyPoints.point: Green energy push: Rs35,000 Cr for solar and hydrogen
keyPoints.detail: The budget allocates Rs35,000 crore for the National Green Hydrogen Mission and solar manufacturing PLI. India targets 500 GW renewable capacity by 2030.

keyPoints.point: Semiconductor Mission gets Rs76,000 Cr top-up
keyPoints.detail: The India Semiconductor Mission corpus was increased to Rs76,000 crore, supporting Tata Dholera fab and Micron Sanand plant. First chips expected by Q4 2026.

keyPoints.point: MSME credit guarantee scheme expanded to Rs5 Cr
keyPoints.detail: The credit guarantee limit for MSMEs was doubled to Rs5 crore, benefiting 63 lakh small businesses. Interest subvention of 2% for exporters was also extended.
Import duties were reduced on 35 categories including EV components, medical devices, and solar equipment. This is expected to reduce input costs for manufacturers by 8-12%.

timeline.event: Budget presented - Nifty hits all-time high of 26,277
Bond yields fall 40bps as fiscal deficit target maintained
Infrastructure stocks rally 12% - L&T, NTPC, Power Grid lead
Opposition raises concerns over disinvestment target of Rs50,000 Cr
Finance Bill passed with 3 amendments on crypto taxation
New tax regime becomes default - 8 crore taxpayers switch

stakeholders.name: Nirmala Sitharaman
stakeholders.position: Architect of the budget - focused on fiscal consolidation while boosting capex

stakeholders.name: V. Anantha Nageswaran
stakeholders.position: Projected 7.4% GDP growth for FY27, citing budget multiplier effect

stakeholders.name: Shaktikanta Das
stakeholders.position: Welcomed fiscal discipline; hinted at rate cut in April if inflation cooperates

stakeholders.name: CII and FICCI
stakeholders.position: Praised capex push but demanded faster GST refunds and labour law reforms

riskFactors: Disinvestment target of Rs50,000 Cr appears optimistic given PSU market conditions
Global commodity price spike could derail fiscal math

RBI Rate Cut Cycle.headline: RBI Rate Cut Cycle Begins: 25bps Cut Expected in April 2026 as Inflation Cools to 4.8%
RBI Rate Cut Cycle.executiveSummary: After holding the repo rate at 6.5% for eight consecutive meetings, the Reserve Bank of India is widely expected to begin its rate-cutting cycle in April 2026. CPI inflation has moderated to 4.8% from a peak of 6.7%, and core inflation is at a 4-year low of 3.9%. The MPC voted 4-2 to hold in February, with two members dissenting in favour of an immediate cut. Bond markets have already priced in 75bps of cuts over the next 12 months, with the 10-year G-Sec yield falling to 6.82%.

keyPoints.point: Food inflation is the last hurdle - vegetable prices up 28% YoY
keyPoints.detail: Tomato and onion prices remain elevated due to erratic monsoon. RBI needs food inflation to cool before cutting. IMD forecasts normal rabi harvest which should ease prices by March.

keyPoints.point: Core inflation at 3.9% gives RBI room to act
keyPoints.detail: Core inflation excluding food and fuel has fallen to 3.9% - the lowest since 2019. This gives the MPC confidence that demand-side inflation is under control.

keyPoints.point: Real rates at 1.7% are restrictive - growth is being sacrificed
keyPoints.detail: With repo at 6.5% and CPI at 4.8%, real rates are 1.7% - above the neutral rate of 1-1.2%. This is slowing credit growth and private investment.

keyPoints.point: Fed rate path is the external constraint
keyPoints.detail: The US Fed has cut rates twice in 2025. If the dollar weakens further, RBI has more room to cut without risking rupee depreciation.

keyPoints.point: Debt mutual funds are the biggest beneficiary of rate cuts
keyPoints.detail: A 75bps rate cut cycle would generate 6-8% capital gains on long-duration debt funds over 12 months, on top of the 7% coupon.

timeline.event: RBI raises repo rate to 6.5% - peak of the hiking cycle
First hold - MPC signals pause as inflation moderates
Two MPC members vote for cut - dissent signals shift in stance
Stance changed from withdrawal of accommodation to neutral
Hold maintained but April cut strongly signalled by Governor
Expected: First 25bps cut - repo rate to 6.25%

stakeholders.name: Shaktikanta Das
stakeholders.position: Signalled April cut if inflation cooperates - last mile of disinflation is the hardest

stakeholders.name: Michael Patra
stakeholders.position: Hawkish - voted to hold in last 3 meetings, concerned about food inflation persistence

stakeholders.name: Ashima Goyal
stakeholders.position: Dovish - has voted for cut in last 2 meetings, argues real rates are too restrictive

stakeholders.name: SBI and HDFC Bank
stakeholders.position: Waiting for RBI cut to reduce MCLR - home loan rates expected to fall 50bps by Dec 2026

riskFactors: El Nino impact on kharif crop could spike food inflation again
Crude oil above 90 per barrel would widen CAD and weaken rupee
US Fed reversing course on cuts would constrain RBI room

Adani Group Controversy.headline: Adani Group: From Hindenburg Crisis to Rs20 Lakh Crore Recovery
Adani Group Controversy.executiveSummary: The Adani Group has staged one of the most remarkable corporate recoveries in Indian history. After the Hindenburg Research short-seller report in January 2023 wiped out Rs10 lakh crore in market cap, the group has rebuilt to Rs20 lakh crore by March 2026. The Supreme Court-appointed expert committee found no regulatory failure by SEBI. GQG Partners invested 1.87 billion dollars in March 2023, signalling institutional confidence. The group has since won India largest solar tender and completed the Ambuja Cements acquisition.

dataHighlights.context: Largest single FII bet on Adani

keyPoints.point: Hindenburg report alleged stock manipulation and accounting fraud
keyPoints.detail: The January 2023 report by Hindenburg Research alleged 88 questions about Adani Group accounting, offshore entities, and stock price manipulation. The group denied all allegations.

keyPoints.point: Supreme Court committee found no SEBI regulatory failure
keyPoints.detail: The expert committee appointed by the Supreme Court in March 2023 submitted its report in May 2023, finding no evidence of regulatory failure by SEBI in monitoring Adani stocks.

keyPoints.point: GQG Partners bet proved prescient - 3x returns in 3 years
keyPoints.detail: GQG Partners invested 1.87 billion dollars across 4 Adani companies in March 2023 at crisis lows. By March 2026, the investment is worth approximately 5.6 billion dollars.

keyPoints.point: Adani Green wins 8 GW solar tender - largest in Indian history
keyPoints.detail: In February 2026, Adani Green Energy won a Rs45,000 crore SECI tender for 8 GW of solar capacity, the largest single renewable energy award in India history.

keyPoints.point: Debt reduction of Rs40,000 crore achieved through asset monetisation
keyPoints.detail: The group sold stakes in Adani Ports, Adani Enterprises, and Adani Total Gas to reduce debt by Rs40,000 crore. Debt/EBITDA has fallen from 7.6x to 3.2x.

timeline.event: Hindenburg Research publishes short-seller report - Rs10 lakh crore wiped out in 3 days
Adani Group withdraws Rs20,000 crore FPO citing market volatility
GQG Partners invests 1.87 billion dollars - first major institutional confidence signal

Adani Group market cap crosses Rs15 lakh crore - near full recovery
Adani Green wins 8 GW SECI tender - largest renewable energy order in India history

stakeholders.name: Gautam Adani
stakeholders.position: Led the recovery strategy - focused on deleveraging, transparency, and winning government contracts

stakeholders.name: Rajiv Jain
stakeholders.position: Made the contrarian 1.87 billion dollar bet in March 2023 - now sitting on 3x returns

stakeholders.name: Madhabi Puri Buch
stakeholders.position: Faced allegations of conflict of interest from Hindenburg in August 2023 - denied all charges

stakeholders.name: Hindenburg Research
stakeholders.position: Published the original short report - dissolved firm in January 2025
